perturbation.original_text: 5. Third Party Disclosure. Both parties agree that they are also obligated to maintain Non Disclosure Agreements and protect the confidentiality of shared information beyond the scope of the individual company should it be required to disclose this information to complete the task required. Therefore, both parties agree to have Non Disclosure Agreements in place with any current or future employee, any third party person or company to the magnitude of this agreement or greater prior to any internal or external disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Third Party Disclosure. Both parties agree that they are not obligated to maintain Non Disclosure Agreements and protect the confidentiality of shared information beyond the scope of the individual company should it be required to disclose this information to complete the task required. Therefore, both parties agree to not have Non Disclosure Agreements in place with any current or future employee, any third party person or company to the magnitude of this agreement or greater prior to any internal or external disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'are obligated' to 'are not obligated', and 'agree to have' to 'agree to not have' creates a contradiction, because now the parties do not need NDAs in place. In the event that the other party breaches section 6 (Duty to Protect/Publicity), the damaged party will not be able to prove or enforce any breaches due to the third party disclosure modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Duty to Protect/Publicity. The Receiving Party shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. The Receiving Party (i) shall limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees, agents, representatives, consultants and contractors who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above and to third party vendors for the purpose of obtaining price quotations, (ii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iii) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons are aware of, understand their obligations under, and comply with the provisions of this Agreement. Each party agrees not to publicize or disclose the existence or terms of this Agreement to any third party without the prior consent of the other party except as required by law (in which case, the party seeking to disclose the information shall give reasonable notice to the other party of its intent to make such a disclosure). Neither party shall make any press release or similar public statement without the prior consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that parties are 'not obligated' to maintain NDAs, directly contradicting Section 6, which places a 'Duty to Protect' confidential information. The removal of the NDA requirement undermines the Receiving Party's ability to 'protect the disclosed Confidential Information', creating a clear and direct conflict. This makes Section 6 unenforceable, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Ownership/ Return of Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party shall remain the owner of all Confidential Information it discloses. Within ten (10) days after any written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Ownership/ Return of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall become the owner of all Confidential Information it discloses. Within ten (10) days after any written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the ownership to the Receiving Party in the first sentence, this contradicts with the fact that the Receiving party must return copies of the Confidential Information. The contradiction creates uncertainty about who the information belongs to after it is disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Ownership/ Return of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall become the owner of all Confidential Information it discloses. Within ten (10) days after any written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "The Receiving Party shall become the owner of all Confidential Information it discloses" which directly contradicts the subsequent requirement in the same section that "the Receiving Party shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information." Ownership and return of property are mutually exclusive, leading to a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but this doesn't negate the contradiction created by the change relative to the original text's meaning.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to California while stating that Texas courts have exclusive jurisdiction creates a direct conflict. The contract now indicates that California law governs the agreement, but Texas courts are responsible for settling disputes. The contradiction creates uncertainty about which laws and court systems will apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that the state courts of Collin or Dallas Counties, Texas and the federal courts located in the Eastern District of Texas shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Texas to California. However, the jurisdiction clause still states that Texas courts have exclusive jurisdiction. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the same section of the contract. The contract indicates California law governs, but Texas courts settle disputes, violating the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party may be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are not necessary or reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages are adequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may not cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party is not entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: Changing key assertions about the necessity of obligations and adequacy of remedies creates a direct contradiction. By asserting that obligations are 'not necessary or reasonable' and monetary damages are 'adequate,' it contradicts the premise that irreparable injury could occur. This change undermines the Disclosing Party's ability to seek injunctive relief, conflicting with the intention of the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Remedies. The Receiving Party agrees that its obligations hereunder are not necessary or reasonable in order to protect the Disclosing Party and the Disclosing Party’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages are adequate to compensate the Disclosing Party for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation may not cause irreparable injury to the Disclosing Party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Disclosing Party is not entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement. The original text states obligations are 'necessary and reasonable' and monetary damages 'may be inadequate,' allowing for injunctive relief. The changed text asserts the opposite: obligations are 'not necessary or reasonable,' monetary damages are 'adequate,' and injunctive relief is 'not entitled.' This is a direct conflict regarding the availability and appropriateness of remedies, making them incompatible. Furthermore, this aligns directly with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition as a clause is placed in what is essentially the same location (clause 11) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either party without the written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Assignment. This Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either party with the written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'Neither' at the beginning of the sentence results in a double negative, and a contradiction. Previously, the rights and obligations could not be assigned without consent. Removing 'Neither' makes it sound like they *can* be assigned with consent. The phrase 'with the written consent of the other party' now reads as a condition that *allows* assignment, not restricts it. The contradiction creates uncertainty, about the ability to assign.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. Assignment. This Agreement nor any rights or obligations hereunder shall be transferred or assigned by either party with the written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement could *not* be assigned without written consent ('Neither this Agreement nor...shall be transferred...without the written consent...'). The changed text removes 'Neither', creating the opposite meaning; it now implies that the agreement *can* be assigned with written consent ('This Agreement nor...shall be transferred...with the written consent...'). The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the assignment clause and also contradicts itself by the word 'nor', creating a strong in-text contradiction based on Omissions - In Text Contradiction and Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. This is also because the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location.
